title: Schoenoplectus jingmenensis
taxonomy: Schoenoplectus jingmenensis is a species of flowering plant in the sedge family, Cyperaceae. It is a member of the genus Schoenoplectus, which is a group of plants commonly known as bulrushes or clubrushes. The genus Schoenoplectus is classified in the family Cyperaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The family Cyperaceae is a part of the order Poales, which includes various monocotyledonous plants such as grasses and sedges. The order Poales is a subgroup of the class Liliopsida, which encompasses a wide range of flowering plant families.

intro: Schoenoplectus jingmenensis, also known as Jingmen bulrush, is a type of aquatic plant belonging to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). Traditionally, it has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of Schoenoplectus jingmenensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Jingmen bulrush is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into herbal formulas to treat a range of health issues.
